overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, including military incidents in the US-Iran conflict, domestic US legal and political developments, FIFA disciplinary and financial news, ongoing violence in Gaza, a sports controversy in Africa, a cartel raid in Mexico, and a US commission approving a commemorative coin featuring Donald Trump.

articles.title: US F-35 aircraft makes emergency landing after a combat mission over Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing at a Middle East airbase after a combat mission over Iran, with the pilot reported in stable condition. US Central Command confirmed the landing is under investigation, while CNN reported the aircraft may have been hit by Iran, and Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed it targeted a US aircraft. The article also provides context on US war objectives and casualty figures since the conflict began on February 28.

articles.title: US states file lawsuit challenging Trump’s revocation of climate finding | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A coalition of 23 US states, led by California and New York, has filed a legal petition challenging the Trump administration's revocation of the 'endangerment finding,' a key scientific determination that climate change threatens human health and the environment. The lawsuit, filed in the US Court of Appeals, also challenges the repeal of tailpipe emissions standards for certain vehicle model years. State officials accuse the administration of choosing 'Big Oil profits over our health' and denying the reality of climate change.

articles.title: FIFA fines Israel FA citing racism and ‘multiple’ discrimination breaches | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA has fined the Israeli Football Association (IFA) 150,000 Swiss francs for multiple breaches of its anti-discrimination obligations, citing a failure to take meaningful action against club Beitar Jerusalem for 'persistent and well-documented racist behaviour.' The disciplinary committee highlighted racist slogans and chants by Beitar fans as part of a systemic pattern. In a separate investigation, FIFA took no action against the IFA regarding Israeli clubs based in the occupied West Bank participating in its leagues.

articles.title: FIFA details Infantino’s $6m deal with 33% Club World Cup bonus increase | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA President Gianni Infantino received a 33% increase in his annual bonus in 2025, part of a $6 million pay package, following the first monthlong men's Club World Cup. His basic salary remained unchanged, but his bonus rose to 2.2 million Swiss francs. FIFA also published accounts targeting $14 billion in revenue for the 2027-30 cycle, which includes future World Cups and Club World Cup editions, with plans to allocate $2.7 billion in development funds.

articles.title: Israel continues Gaza attacks amid regional war, kills several Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes in Gaza City killed at least three Palestinians and wounded others, according to doctors at al-Shifa Hospital. The attacks occurred as the Rafah border crossing with Egypt reopened for limited medical evacuations after a 19-day closure by Israel. The Palestinian Red Crescent Society stated only eight wounded Palestinians and 17 family members would be allowed into Egypt for treatment on Thursday.

articles.title: Senegal and Morocco tied by religion and trade but divided by AFCON fallout | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) overturned Senegal's Africa Cup of Nations victory and awarded the title to Morocco, a decision met with disbelief in Senegal. Senegal's government is pursuing legal avenues and called for an investigation into suspected corruption, while its football federation is preparing a case for the Court of Arbitration for Sport. The controversy strains the close ties between the two nations, which are built on shared religion, trade, and culture.

articles.title: Mexican military says 11 killed in raid targeting Sinaloa cartel leader | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mexican naval forces killed 11 people during a raid in Culiacan that captured Omar Oswaldo Torres, leader of the Los Mayos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel. The navy stated its personnel were attacked and returned fire, seizing high-powered weapons at the scene. The operation is part of broader regional efforts against cartels, though experts warn such 'decapitation strategies' can increase long-term violence and fail to address root causes like poverty and corruption.

articles.title: US arts commission approves gold coin stamped with Donald Trump’s face | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Commission of Fine Arts, composed of Trump appointees, unanimously approved a commemorative gold coin featuring a portrait of Donald Trump. The coin may sidestep a federal law prohibiting living presidents on currency, as it is commemorative and not for circulation. Critics, including a US senator, denounced the move as unlawful and akin to actions by 'monarchs and dictators,' while the administration also plans to put Trump's face on a $1 coin.

overall_summary: This batch of articles covers a range of international news from the last 6 hours, including US domestic issues like a Homeland Security shutdown and a climate lawsuit, developments in the US-Iran conflict, FIFA governance stories, and regional reports from Gaza, Africa, and Mexico.

articles.title: Why the US Homeland Security shutdown is raising fears of airport delays | Government News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A partial US government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has left Transportation Security Administration (TSA) workers without pay, leading to resignations and unscheduled leave. This has resulted in staffing shortfalls, causing long lines and delays at airports. The shutdown stems from a political deadlock in Congress, where Democrats are demanding immigration policy changes as a condition for DHS funding, which expired on February 14.

articles.title: US approves $16.5bn arms deal to Gulf states amid rising Iran tensions | Politics News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US State Department has approved a $16.5 billion arms sale to the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Jordan, including drones, missiles, and radar systems. The State Department stated the sale supports US national security objectives and was approved under an emergency provision, bypassing congressional approval. This deal occurs amid heightened tensions and ongoing conflict between the US, its allies, and Iran, which has impacted global energy prices.

articles.title: US F-35 aircraft makes emergency landing after a combat mission over Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing at a Middle East airbase after a combat mission over Iran; the pilot is reported to be in stable condition. US Central Command confirmed the landing and stated the incident is under investigation, while reports suggest the aircraft may have been hit by Iran. The article also notes US combat losses in the ongoing conflict with Iran, which began on February 28.

articles.title: US states file lawsuit challenging Trump’s revocation of climate finding | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A coalition of 23 US states, led by California and New York, has filed a legal petition challenging the Trump administration's revocation of the 'endangerment finding,' a key scientific determination that climate change threatens human health and the environment. The lawsuit, filed in the US Court of Appeals, argues the repeal unlawfully removes the basis for federal climate regulations. The states accuse the administration of choosing 'Big Oil profits over our health' and undermining environmental protections.

articles.title: FIFA fines Israel FA citing racism and ‘multiple’ discrimination breaches | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA has fined the Israeli Football Association (IFA) 150,000 Swiss francs for multiple breaches of its anti-discrimination obligations, following an investigation prompted by a Palestinian FA complaint. The disciplinary committee found the IFA failed to take meaningful action against Beitar Jerusalem for persistent racist behavior by its fans. The IFA was also ordered to display an anti-discrimination banner at matches and invest part of the fine in a prevention plan, while FIFA took no action regarding Israeli clubs based in the occupied West Bank.

articles.title: FIFA details Infantino’s $6m deal with 33% Club World Cup bonus increase | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA's published accounts reveal President Gianni Infantino received a $6 million pay package for 2025, including a 33% increase in his annual bonus to 2.2 million Swiss francs, linked to the first men's Club World Cup. FIFA's revenue target for the 2027-30 cycle is $14 billion, which includes funding for future World Cups. Infantino is eligible for re-election next year for a fourth term that could extend his presidency to 2031.

articles.title: Israel continues Gaza attacks amid regional war, kills several Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes in Gaza City killed at least three Palestinians and wounded others, according to local doctors. These attacks occur as the Rafah border crossing with Egypt reopened for the first time in 19 days, allowing a limited medical evacuation of eight wounded Palestinians and 17 family members. Israel's war on Gaza has killed more than 72,000 people, and Israel has restricted medical evacuations despite a prior ceasefire agreement.

articles.title: Senegal and Morocco tied by religion and trade but divided by AFCON fallout | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) overturned Senegal's Africa Cup of Nations victory and awarded the title to Morocco, a decision met with disbelief in Senegal. Senegal's government and football federation are pursuing legal avenues, including a potential appeal to the Court of Arbitration for Sport, alleging suspected corruption. This sporting dispute strains the close historical, religious, and economic ties between the two nations.

articles.title: Mexican military says 11 killed in raid targeting Sinaloa cartel leader | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Mexican Navy reports that 11 people were killed during a raid in Culiacan that captured Omar Oswaldo Torres, a leader of the Los Mayos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel. The navy stated its personnel were attacked and returned fire, seizing weapons and equipment at the scene. The raid is part of a broader, militarized strategy against cartels encouraged by US President Donald Trump, though experts warn such 'decapitation' tactics can increase long-term violence.

articles.title: US arts commission approves gold coin stamped with Donald Trump’s face | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Commission of Fine Arts, comprised of Trump appointees, unanimously approved a commemorative gold coin featuring a portrait of President Donald Trump. The coin may sidestep a federal law prohibiting living presidents on currency because it is commemorative, but the administration is also pursuing a Trump-themed $1 coin. Critics, including a US senator, denounce the move as unlawful and akin to actions taken by monarchs or dictators.
